title: Neonauclea purpurascens
intro: Neonauclea purpurascens, also known as "purplish yellow nauclea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Neonauclea purpurascens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neonauclea purpurascens is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or paste, and is commonly used to make teas, tonics, and topical creams.
